verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 encodes HSP60 (mitochondrial chaperonin), canonically localized to the mitochondrial matrix. However, HSP60 is one of the best-documented 'ecto-chaperones': it is displayed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells (multiple cancer types including prostate, ovarian, leukemia), on activated immune cells, and on cells undergoing immunogenic/apoptotic stress. Surface HSP60 on tumor cells has been validated by flow cytometry and surface biotinylation and is a target for antibody-based therapies and CAR-T strategies. Cell-death-associated and cancer-associated outer-leaflet display is well-documented. Additionally, HSP60 can be shed/secreted and associates non-covalently with membrane surfaces. The dominant compartment is mitochondrial matrix, but the cancer-cell and cell-death ecto-pool is the surface story, qualifying this as contextual / cell_state_induced.
